description_long: This object is a head of broccoli with a thick, pale green stalk and a dense, rounded crown composed of tightly clustered, small green florets. The surface of the crown is bumpy and uneven, with a granular texture typical of fresh broccoli. Several small leaves are attached near the base of the stalk, some curling outward and others pressed against the crown. The stalk is cylindrical, tapering slightly as it extends upward, and shows a hollowed-out cut at the base. The coloration transitions from a yellowish-green at the stalk to a deeper green at the crown, with subtle variations in hue across the surface. The overall form is compact, with the crown forming a dome-like shape and the stalk providing structural support.
description: A compact, dome-shaped broccoli head with a thick, pale stalk and tightly clustered green florets. Small leaves are attached near the base, and the surface is bumpy and granular.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thick stalk and the rounded, bumpy crown with attached leaves.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the dense, granular floret crown and overall dome shape.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the stalk and crown visible, as well as more pronounced leaf attachments.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the hollowed-out base of the stalk and the underside of the crown.
attribute_floret_clustering: Florets are densely packed across the crown, with minor variation in size and minimal visible spacing between clusters.
attribute_stalk_segmentation: Stalk appears mostly continuous with a single prominent cut at the base and no clear segmentation into nodes.
attribute_branching_pattern: Branching is irregular, with a few primary branches extending from the main stalk and supporting the crown; secondary branching is minimal and asymmetrical.
attribute_leaf_attachment: Several small leaves are attached near the base of the stalk, oriented outward and downward; leaves are not distributed along the stalk.
attribute_crown_density: Crown is solid and highly dense, with little to no visible gaps or open areas within the floret mass.
